flashcards.definition: Doubling time = 70 ÷ growth rate (in percent); quick estimation of how fast a population doubles.
Per capita rate of population change; r = birth rate − death rate (for closed populations).
Maximum population growth rate occurs at N = K/2 (inflection point of the S-curve).
ΔN = (births + immigration) − (deaths + emigration); comprehensive population accounting.
Time for a population to double in size; shorter doubling time = faster growth.
